jokes.text: Leslie suggesting they order 200 white-chocolate top hats for the wedding
jokes.setup: Ben mentions they've already made wedding preparations
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and impractical wedding favor choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie Knope character moment - her tendency to over-plan with bizarre specificity. The white-chocolate top hats are both absurd and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: July 57th
jokes.setup: Ben listing possible wedding dates
jokes.punchline: Including an impossible date that doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Quick absurdist throwaway that works because it's delivered naturally within the romantic moment.

jokes.text: Ben wanting Leslie to take his last name and become 'Councilwoman Mrs. Ben Wyatt'
jokes.setup: Ben seriously discussing taking his last name
jokes.punchline: The absurdly formal and patriarchal title suggestion
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke that plays with gender expectations before revealing Ben is just messing with her.

jokes.text: Ben asking 'Where do I go?' after Leslie tells him to run
jokes.setup: Leslie dramatically tells Ben to run
jokes.punchline: Ben's practical confusion about logistics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's practical nature cutting through Leslie's dramatic energy.

jokes.text: The falcon is entering! The turtledove acknowledges!
jokes.setup: Leslie calling Ann with urgency
jokes.punchline: Using elaborate spy code names for wedding planning
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-dramatization. The spy codes for wedding planning perfectly capture her tendency to treat everything like a mission.

jokes.text: the sensuality of Eleanor Roosevelt combined with the animal magnetism of Lesley Stahl
jokes.setup: Leslie describing her desired wedding look
jokes.punchline: Combining two unlikely and non-glamorous reference points
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leslie character comedy - her attraction to accomplished but unglamorous women in politics/journalism as style icons is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: That guy's my wife
jokes.setup: Tom saying he has a guy at City Hall
jokes.punchline: Revealing his 'guy' is his wife
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and misdirection. The follow-up of him realizing he also works there makes it even better.

jokes.text: Um, you also work there
jokes.setup: Tom's convoluted explanation about having connections
jokes.punchline: Leslie pointing out the obvious
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reality check that deflates Tom's attempt to sound connected.

jokes.text: It would be an honor, and the first time I won't regret walking down the aisle
jokes.setup: Ron agreeing to walk Leslie down the aisle
jokes.punchline: Implying his own marriages were regrettable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment - his dry delivery of personal pain while showing genuine affection for Leslie.

jokes.text: Steve Harvey vibe or straight-up Seacrest... I'll combine 'em-- Steve-crest
jokes.setup: Tom asking about officiating style
jokes.punchline: The awkward portmanteau of Steve-crest
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom attempt at being clever that falls flat in a funny way.

jokes.text: And they were nipple rings, at one point
jokes.setup: Andy and April looking at rings in a pawn shop
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation about the rings' intimate previous use
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor that escalates when we learn they belonged to the clerk himself.

jokes.text: They were his
jokes.setup: Learning the nipple rings belonged to someone
jokes.punchline: April's deadpan revelation they belonged to the male clerk
jokes.explanation: April's deadpan delivery makes the uncomfortable revelation even funnier.

jokes.text: There is some beautiful jewelry in here. But be careful. There's also nails.
jokes.setup: Clerk showing April and Andy jewelry options
jokes.punchline: Casual warning about actual nails mixed in with jewelry
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about Pawnee's low standards - jewelry mixed with construction supplies.

jokes.text: I guess I better commit as many crimes as possible tonight. Get it out of my system
jokes.setup: Andy mentioning he becomes a cop tomorrow
jokes.punchline: His backward logic about committing crimes before becoming a cop
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - completely backwards reasoning delivered with total sincerity.

jokes.text: I don't want anyone else in this town to get married, because it makes it less special for us
jokes.setup: April taking all the marriage licenses
jokes.punchline: Her selfish but oddly romantic reasoning
jokes.explanation: Great April character moment - her weird logic somehow becomes romantic in the context of supporting Leslie and Ben.

jokes.text: If you're not gonna finish something three months early, then don't accept the job
jokes.setup: Finding out the wedding dress isn't finished
jokes.punchline: Leslie's unreasonable expectation of extreme earliness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character trait - her impossible standards for preparedness extending to others.

jokes.text: Squirrels and birds
jokes.setup: Leslie asking how Cinderella finished her dress so fast
jokes.punchline: Ann's literal answer to the fairy tale reference
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Ann's practical response to Leslie's fantasy thinking.

jokes.text: I forge government documents all the time
jokes.setup: Needing official signatures on the marriage license
jokes.punchline: April casually admitting to regular document forgery
jokes.explanation: Classic April dark humor - delivering criminal confession with complete casualness.

jokes.text: I know you're in a rush, Leslie. I should point out that you said 'duty' twice
jokes.setup: Leslie rapidly assigning tasks using the word 'duty'
jokes.punchline: Ron's juvenile observation about the double entendre
jokes.explanation: Funny because it's Ron being unexpectedly juvenile at the most inappropriate moment.

jokes.text: Did you just pee your pants? Just a dab
jokes.setup: Jerry's excited reaction to Li'l Sebastian news
jokes.punchline: Jerry's casual admission to slight incontinence
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry gross-out humor made funnier by his matter-of-fact delivery.

jokes.text: Third time this week
jokes.setup: Jerry admitting to peeing himself
jokes.punchline: Revealing this is a regular occurrence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes Jerry's situation even more pathetic and funny.

jokes.text: I was gonna beatbox. There's a dance breakdown. I'm talking, like, six guaranteed applause breaks
jokes.setup: Tom upset about not being able to officiate
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top performance plans for a wedding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - treating a wedding like a nightclub performance opportunity.

jokes.text: just a tennis bracelet and a necklace that says 'Wine Chick'
jokes.setup: Looking through Ann's jewelry for rings
jokes.punchline: The tacky 'Wine Chick' necklace detail
jokes.explanation: Funny visual detail that suggests Ann's questionable jewelry taste.

jokes.text: Li'l Sebastian? No, no, sorry. Actually, this is Bucky
jokes.setup: Everyone excited thinking they see Li'l Sebastian
jokes.punchline: Deflating revelation it's just an impersonator
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that deflates the moment while advancing the plot.

jokes.text: This is Li'l Sebastian, and this is a sign
jokes.setup: Learning it's just an impersonator
jokes.punchline: Leslie deciding the fake is still a meaningful sign
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie optimism - her ability to find meaning in anything that suits her narrative.

jokes.text: There's always time. Designers, make it work
jokes.setup: Ann saying there's no time for the Tim Gunn voice
jokes.punchline: Immediately doing the Tim Gunn impression anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ann can't resist the reference even under pressure.

jokes.text: I'm not married. I was on a date tonight. It went well, he's sleeping over
jokes.setup: Andy asking if the man in bed is her husband
jokes.punchline: Elderly woman casually mentioning her successful date
jokes.explanation: Unexpected TMI from elderly character that catches everyone off guard.

jokes.text: Is this a ruby that's gone bad?
jokes.setup: Chris examining Ann's jewelry
jokes.punchline: Describing a gemstone like spoiled food
jokes.explanation: Funny absurdist description that treats gemstones like perishable goods.

jokes.text: Is this a ruby that's gone bad?
jokes.setup: Chris examining Ann's jewelry
jokes.punchline: Treating gemstones like spoiled food
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist description treating jewelry like food

jokes.text: How much do you think Ann would miss this sconce?
jokes.setup: Running out of ring options
jokes.punchline: Ron casually suggesting stealing Ann's home fixtures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - practical solution that's also mildly criminal, delivered with total sincerity.

jokes.text: you mad sweet on that back meat
jokes.setup: Tom practicing his wedding speech
jokes.punchline: The awkward phrase 'back meat' for Ben's butt
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom inappropriate humor that makes everyone uncomfortable.

jokes.text: You ever heard Leslie talk about Ben's butt? I'm all like, 'damn, girl, you mad sweet on that back meat.'
jokes.setup: Tom practicing his wedding material
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate and awkward phrasing of 'back meat'
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortably inappropriate humor that's cringeworthy but not cleverly so

jokes.text: Hey, butter-teeth, what's up? I can fix those chompers for you
jokes.setup: Jamm protesting at the gala
jokes.punchline: Insulting someone then immediately trying to sell them services
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jamm character moment - being awful while trying to drum up business.

jokes.text: Councilman Howser
jokes.setup: Jamm calling Leslie names
jokes.punchline: Calling her Doogie Howser for being young
jokes.explanation: Weak reference humor that fits Jamm's character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Terrible. And perfect
jokes.setup: Ben asking how the makeshift dress looks
jokes.punchline: Ron's contradictory but sincere assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron economy of words that captures both honesty and sentiment.

jokes.text: It is the letter from the statehouse... Telling us that we have been assigned to Pawnee. Dated may 1st, 2010.
jokes.setup: Chris giving Ben a meaningful gift
jokes.punchline: The callback to the beginning of their Pawnee assignment
jokes.explanation: Touching callback that reframes the whole series as leading to this moment

jokes.text: It is like the Ann Perkins of dresses
jokes.setup: Leslie examining the dress Ann made
jokes.punchline: Using Ann as the ultimate compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Leslie's Ann worship, used as the highest possible praise.

jokes.text: I'm not supposed to see you before the wedding. Well, I saw him too
jokes.setup: Andy worried about wedding superstitions
jokes.punchline: His illogical solution to cover all bases
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy logic - if one superstition is bad, all seeing must be equally bad.

jokes.text: Here comes the boo, All dressed in boo, Boo, Leslie Knope, Boo, boo-oo-oo-oo
jokes.setup: Jamm disrupting the wedding
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt at a protest song
jokes.explanation: Funny because of how pathetic and poorly executed Jamm's protest is.

jokes.text: ♪ Here comes the boo ♪ ♪ All dressed in boo ♪ ♪ Boo, Leslie Knope ♪ ♪ Boo, boo-oo-oo-oo ♪
jokes.setup: Jamm disrupting the wedding processional
jokes.punchline: His pathetic anti-wedding song parody
jokes.explanation: Appropriately pathetic for Jamm but not particularly clever or funny

jokes.text: Amazon, baby, that's how. You are looking at a prime customer, everybody. Two days free shipping for this guy
jokes.setup: Someone asking where he got stink bombs
jokes.punchline: Jamm bragging about his Amazon Prime membership
jokes.explanation: Funny because Jamm treats Amazon Prime like a major life achievement during a criminal act.

jokes.text: Well, good thing you're an orthodontist
jokes.setup: Jamm complaining about his broken tooth
jokes.punchline: Leslie's sarcastic observation about his profession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie comeback - turning Jamm's complaint into an observation about his luck.

jokes.text: You broke my tooth. Well, good thing you're an orthodontist.
jokes.setup: Ron punching Jamm in the mouth
jokes.punchline: The irony that an orthodontist broke his tooth
jokes.explanation: Good ironic punchline delivered with perfect Ron deadpan

jokes.text: I don't think you're supposed to punch a man in the mouth during the service. I think that's exactly what you're supposed to do, if that mouth is attached to a drunk jerk-face.
jokes.setup: Ron apologizing for punching Jamm
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate defense and reframing of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie logic defending Ron's violence as wedding appropriate

jokes.text: Ken Burns never wrote me back
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why Leslie is important to him
jokes.punchline: Including Ken Burns in his list of absent father figures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron character moment - treating the documentarian like a potential father figure is both absurd and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: He chopped off both of his arms, so he wouldn't leave any fingerprints
jokes.setup: Andy describing a criminal case
jokes.punchline: The illogical method of avoiding fingerprints
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy police fantasy - completely illogical but delivered with total confidence.

jokes.text: Well, how did he murder the person?
jokes.setup: Andy's story about armless criminal
jokes.punchline: The chief asking the obvious logical question
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that highlights the absurdity of Andy's logic.

jokes.text: I've never willingly been here later than 5:04 p.m.
jokes.setup: Chris commenting on being at City Hall at night
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific time cutoff
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's precision and work-life boundaries.

jokes.text: I love the first floor of City Hall at night. When all the fluorescents are half-off. Just makes the informational fliers look so beautiful.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing City Hall at night
jokes.punchline: Finding beauty in the most mundane government office details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - finding genuine beauty in government bureaucracy

jokes.text: Yes, just do it already
jokes.setup: Jerry suggesting they keep the ceremony short
jokes.punchline: April's blunt agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - cutting through ceremony with characteristic bluntness.

jokes.text: The first draft of my vows, which I wrote the day after we got engaged, clocked in at around 70 pages
jokes.setup: Leslie starting her vows
jokes.punchline: Revealing her original vows were insanely long
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her tendency to over-prepare and over-write everything.

jokes.text: the first draft of my vows, which I wrote the day after we got engaged, clocked in at around 70 pages.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her vows situation
jokes.punchline: The absurd length of her original vows
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie over-preparation taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: I love you and I like you.
jokes.setup: Leslie's improvised vows
jokes.punchline: The simple but meaningful distinction between love and like
jokes.explanation: Series-defining line that perfectly captures Leslie's philosophy about relationships

jokes.text: Close only counts in horse grenades
jokes.setup: Andy trying to use an idiom
jokes.punchline: Completely mangling 'horseshoes and hand grenades'
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy malapropism that creates an absurd mental image.

jokes.text: It's from the movie Seabiscuit
jokes.setup: Andy's horse grenade explanation
jokes.punchline: Attributing it to the wrong movie entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Andy confidently attributing his made-up violent horse saying to a heartwarming horse movie.

jokes.text: Any moron with a crucible, an acetylene torch, and a cast-iron waffle maker could have done the same
jokes.setup: Ron describing his ring-making process
jokes.punchline: Calling it simple while listing extremely specialized equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - casually dismissing an incredibly complex process while listing equipment most people don't own. The waffle maker detail is especially perfect.

jokes.text: Any moron with a crucible, an acetylene torch, and a cast-iron waffle maker could have done the same.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his ring-making process
jokes.punchline: Calling it simple while listing extremely specialized equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - treating extremely complex craftsmanship as basic common sense
